spec_match.rationale: Actually uses the native CSS alpha() function for state colors, allowing the browser to resolve it.

interactive.rationale: Highly interactive, allowing the brand color to be changed dynamically, showcasing how alpha() automatically derives all states from a single custom property.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The blurb and demo align perfectly, demonstrating the exact value proposition of deriving UI state colors from a single brand token.

edge_cases.rationale: The support note claims an oklch fallback exists, but there are no actual fallback rules in the CSS, leading to invisible backgrounds in unsupported browsers.

references.rationale: Cites ChromeStatus and CSS Color 5 spec.

design_system.rationale: Follows design system conventions, including proper button color overrides.

openQuestions.title: Implement actual CSS fallbacks for unsupported browsers
openQuestions.detail: The support note claims a fallback is used, but the CSS lacks any fallback rules. Add a robust fallback like color-mix(in srgb, var(--brand) 12%, transparent) before the alpha() declaration to ensure the UI remains usable in non-supporting browsers.
